authority.kind: Lean declarations at pinned git commits

verified_results.signature_summary: {V : Type u} -> [Fintype V] -> [DecidableEq V] -> (D : Nat) -> (H : SimpleGraph V) -> [DecidableRel H.Adj] -> (J : Finset (Sym2 V)) -> InAuxiliaryClass D H J -> HasValidRainbowColoring D H J
verified_results.scope: arbitrary finite vertex type and every formal member of InAuxiliaryClass
verified_results.conclusion_expansion: There exists a proper edge assignment on H with colors Fin (D + 2) whose colors are pairwise distinct on distinguishedEdgeSet H J.

verified_results.signature_summary: {V : Type u} -> {W : Type v} -> {G : SimpleGraph V} -> {H : SimpleGraph W} -> [Fintype W] -> [DecidableEq W] -> (D : Nat) -> (X : Auxiliary.Extension G H) -> [DecidableRel H.Adj] -> (J : Finset (Sym2 W)) -> (forall vertex : V, X.classEdge vertex in distinguishedEdgeSet H J) -> InAuxiliaryClass D H J -> exists assignment : Assignment G (ExtensionPalette D), assignment.Valid
verified_results.scope: arbitrary original graph G with a supplied conflict-preserving extension into a finite formal auxiliary-class member H and explicit selector membership in J
verified_results.output_kind: propositional existence of a semantically valid total-coloring assignment
verified_results.conclusion_expansion: There exists assignment : Assignment G (Fin (D + 2)) with assignment.Valid; the theorem does not construct the extension or identify D with the maximum degree of G.

verified_results.signature_summary: {V : Type u} -> {G : SimpleGraph V} -> [Fintype V] -> [DecidableEq V] -> (P : Auxiliary.PairSingletonWitness G) -> (v : V) -> P.extension.classEdge v in distinguishedEdgeSet P.auxiliaryGraph P.distinguished
verified_results.scope: a supplied PairSingletonWitness on a finite original vertex type
verified_results.output_kind: concrete auxiliary graph and conflict-preserving Extension data, selector membership, qualitative auxiliary-class structure, and conditional structural-class membership
verified_results.conditional_class_conclusion: IsAuxiliaryClassMember D P.auxiliaryGraph none P.distinguished P.matchingPart
conditional_class_hypotheses: 2 <= P.auxiliaryGraph.degree none and P.auxiliaryGraph.degree none <= D
verified_results.conclusion_expansion: P.auxiliaryGraph is defined on Option V; P.extension is an Auxiliary.Extension G P.auxiliaryGraph; every selector lies in the corresponding distinguishedEdgeSet; each copied original vertex has exact distinguished-edge coverage; matchingPart is an off-center matching whose endpoints avoid center neighbors; and distinguished is matchingPart union the full center incidence star. The conditional theorem isAuxiliaryClassMember_of_numeric supplies IsAuxiliaryClassMember only after the three listed numerical hypotheses are provided. It does not construct the witness, prove those numerical hypotheses, identify D with a parameter of G, or prove an end-to-end total-coloring theorem.

not_proved: an unrestricted total-coloring theorem for all finite graph orders
the proposed high-degree total-coloring theorem with Delta + 2 colors
the proposed high-degree total-coloring theorem with Delta + 3 colors
the equitable-partition input and construction of a PairSingletonWitness from it
P.distinguished.card = D for the supplied witness
P.auxiliaryGraph.maxDegree <= D for the supplied witness
2 <= P.auxiliaryGraph.degree none and P.auxiliaryGraph.degree none <= D for the supplied witness
an end-to-end corollary from an arbitrary input graph
any identification of D with the maximum degree of G
trust_boundary: Lean 4.32.0 kernel checking is authoritative for the formal declarations.
Production Lean modules prohibit sorry, admit, custom axiom declarations, and native_decide.
Executable certificate checkers have semantic soundness theorems.
External JSON parsing and claims of census completeness remain outside the trusted Lean interface.
A checked positive assignment validates that assignment; it does not prove an external enumeration complete.
